199.reason: [Returning false here terminates the lexical analysis prematurely. Instead, after recording the error, the lexer should continue processing the rest of the input to ensure correct line number tracking and to handle the entire file, even though the problem states there's only one error. More critically, returning false causes the main loop to exit, but the lexer hasn't necessarily reached EOF, which violates the contract of the next() method (which should return false only at EOF). The correct approach is to skip the erroneous character and then continue to the next token within the same call or subsequent calls, not terminate the entire analysis.]
